title: 2 Software Stocks to Buy Before the Anthropic IPO
author: Adam Spatacco
description: Anthropic, the developer of Claude AI models, has filed for an IPO after raising $65 billion. Rather than waiting for the IPO, investors can gain exposure to Anthropic's growth through Salesforce and Zoom, which hold significant equity stakes and have integrated Claude into their core platforms. Both companies benefit from Anthropic's advancements through improved product capabilities, stronger user engagement, and opportunities to upsell premium features.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Holds a $5 billion stake in Anthropic and has deeply integrated Claude into its Einstein AI, Agentforce, and Slack platforms. Benefits from improved AI capabilities, higher user engagement, and opportunities for premium feature upsells.

Invested $97 million in Anthropic with stake now valued at $1.3 billion. Integrated Claude into AI Companion and Contact Center suite, positioning it to benefit from Anthropic's growth and improving its core collaboration offerings.

title: Want to Invest in Anthropic? Here's the Sneaky Stock to Buy.
author: Geoffrey Seiler
description: Anthropic, a rapidly growing AI company, is preparing for an IPO at a valuation over $900 billion. While direct investment isn't yet possible, Zoom Communications offers indirect exposure through its $1.27 billion stake in Anthropic (valued at over $3 billion at current valuations). Zoom's core business is also showing growth with improving enterprise metrics and strong cash generation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Core business showing signs of recovery with 5.5% revenue growth and 7.2% enterprise revenue growth. Significant unrealized gains on $1.27 billion Anthropic investment (valued at $3+ billion), strong balance sheet with $7.7 billion cash and no debt, plus $2 billion annual free cash flow generation.

title: Tropic Launches New Intelligence Hub and Brings Its Procurement Data Inside ChatGPT
author: Not Specified
description: Tropic launched the Intelligence Hub, a free resource providing proprietary spend intelligence and negotiation data from $21B+ in managed spend, alongside a ChatGPT app integration. The platform reveals that AI-native vendors are gaining significant market share with 131% NDR in April 2026, while legacy SaaS providers are losing ground, particularly in the SMB segment where AI-native spending grew 82% year-over-year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Emphasizing Primary Provider Clause requiring 70-90% of customer traffic as leverage for pricing negotiations, indicating aggressive contract terms
Mentioned as example in ChatGPT app context; no specific negative or positive news about the company itself
Referenced as example for negotiation tactics; no specific company performance data provided

title: S&P 500 Rally Defies Weak Sentiment and Hawkish Fed Signals
author: Itai Smidt
description: The S&P 500 rallied to new highs despite record-low consumer sentiment, hawkish Fed signals, and geopolitical tensions. The market is experiencing a broad rotation from mega-cap AI stocks to semiconductor suppliers, quantum computing names, and space-launch companies. Quantum computing stocks surged following a $2 billion Commerce Department investment, while chip suppliers and AI infrastructure plays outperformed. Yields retreated from recent highs, providing relief to equity multiples and enabling the rally to extend.

title: Dow Jones Hits Record Highs, DELL Jumps 15%: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks reached record highs on Friday with the Dow Jones Industrial Average surpassing 50,610, driven by a powerful AI-led rally in semiconductor and PC stocks. Dell Technologies surged 15% to a record high following Morgan Stanley's price target increase citing a $43 billion AI server backlog. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 are on track for their eighth consecutive weekly gain, though consumer sentiment hit a record low amid rising inflation expectations and geopolitical tensions affecting oil prices.
